hx_brief: 62yo male with sudden onset tearing chest pain radiating to the back.

hints.hx: History of uncontrolled hypertension. Patient describes pain as 10/10.
hints.pe: Blood pressure 180/100 in right arm, 140/90 in left arm. Diminished femoral pulses.

hx_brief: 24yo female with periumbilical pain that migrated to the RLQ.

hints.hx: Nausea and one episode of vomiting. Appetite is gone (anorexia).
hints.pe: Tenderness at McBurney's point. Positive Rovsing sign.

hx_brief: 55yo female with sudden onset of severe 'pounding' headache and blurry vision.

hints.hx: Patient has a history of hypertension. Reports seeing 'halos' around lights.
hints.pe: Right eye is red. Pupil is mid-dilated and non-reactive to light. Eye feels 'rock hard'.
hints.lab: Normal ESR. Elevated intraocular pressure (IOP) on tonometry.
hints.img: Slit-lamp exam shows shallow anterior chamber.

hx_brief: 32yo male with 2 days of progressive weakness in his legs and tingling in his feet.

hints.hx: Had a diarrheal illness 2 weeks ago (Campylobacter). Weakness is moving up his body.
hints.pe: Symmetric ascending muscle weakness. Absent deep-tendon reflexes (areflexia).
hints.lab: CSF analysis shows high protein but normal WBC count (albuminocytologic dissociation).
hints.img: Nerve conduction studies show slowed conduction velocities.

hx_brief: 68yo male with painless gross hematuria (blood in urine) for three weeks.

hints.hx: 50-pack-year smoking history. Worked in a chemical factory for 30 years.
hints.pe: No flank pain. No suprapubic tenderness. Prostate is normal on digital rectal exam.
hints.lab: Urinalysis confirms 4+ blood. Negative for nitrites or leukocyte esterase.
hints.img: Cystoscopy reveals a papillary mass on the bladder wall.

hx_brief: 45yo obese female with RUQ pain that radiates to her right shoulder after eating a burger.

hints.hx: Nausea and bloating. This has happened before, but this time the pain won't stop.
hints.pe: Positive Murphy’s sign. Guarding in the right upper quadrant.
hints.lab: Elevated WBC (14,000). Mildly elevated Alkaline Phosphatase.
hints.img: Ultrasound shows gallbladder wall thickening and pericholecystic fluid.

hx_brief: 19yo male athlete with sudden collapse and death during a basketball game.

hints.hx: Uncle died suddenly at age 25. No previous medical history of asthma or heart disease.
hints.pe: Harsh systolic murmur that increases in intensity with Valsalva maneuver.
hints.lab: Genetic testing often shows mutations in the myosin heavy chain.
hints.img: Echo shows asymmetric septal hypertrophy and systolic anterior motion of the mitral valve.

hx_brief: 29yo female with 3 days of 'crushing' fatigue, pale skin, and dark yellow urine.

hints.hx: Just finished a course of nitrofurantoin for a UTI. Reports eating fava beans yesterday.
hints.pe: Slightly icteric (yellow) sclera. Splenomegaly is absent.
hints.lab: Low hemoglobin. Low haptoglobin. High indirect bilirubin.
hints.img: Peripheral blood smear shows Heinz bodies and Bite cells.

hx_brief: 72yo female with sudden 'worst headache of my life' and neck stiffness.

hints.hx: History of polycystic kidney disease (ADPKD). No history of trauma.
hints.pe: Positive Kernig and Brudzinski signs. Ptosis of the left eye (CN III palsy).
hints.lab: Lumbar puncture shows xanthochromia (yellowish CSF from old blood).
hints.img: Non-contrast CT of the head shows white 'starfish' pattern in the basal cisterns.
